channel.title: A day in the life of an Entrepreneur on Vandana Verma
channel.description: Recent content in A day in the life of an Entrepreneur on Vandana Verma

item.title: SERIES 2 - A Day in the Life of a Security Leader: Between Alerts, Boardrooms, and Chaos
item.description:
A Day in the Life of a Security Leader: Between Alerts, Boardrooms, and Chaos

A conversation about what a modern security leader’s day really looks like - juggling alerts, board conversations, incident response, team leadership, and strategic priorities. Part of Series 2 of the “A Day in the Life of an Entrepreneur” / Security Leader series.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qhmg3vNJRR0

item.title: SERIES 2 - A Day in the Life of an Entrepreneur - Anant Shrivastava
item.description:
Anant Shrivastava

Security researcher, trainer, and founder of Cyfinoid Research. Anant shares his journey from offensive security research into training and founding his own company, and what it looks like to build an independent research-led security business.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=d1RU5eLO47U

item.title: SERIES 2 - A Day in the Life of an Entrepreneur - Ajit Hatti
item.description:
Ajit Hatti

Security researcher, community builder, and entrepreneur. Ajit shares his journey across security research, co-founding community efforts, and running a security business, along with the lessons he has learned about resilience as an entrepreneur.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6ZQRtmIHKQc

item.title: SERIES 2 - A Day in the Life of an Entrepreneur - Subho Halder
item.description:
Subho Halder - Appknox

Co-founder and CISO at Appknox. Subho shares his journey into mobile application security entrepreneurship, the challenges of building a product business from India for the world, and his perspective on leadership and growth as a founder.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k3TFiVrgIjw

item.title: SERIES 2 - A Day in the Life of an Entrepreneur - Guy Podjarny
item.description:
Guy Podjarny - Snyk

Founder of Snyk and long-time application security leader. Guy shares his journey from AppSec engineer and author to founding Snyk, and what building a developer-first security company has taught him about entrepreneurship, leadership, and the future of DevSecOps.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NUyvwCswcZ4

item.title: SERIES 2 - A Day in the Life of a Security Leader - Kamal Sharma
item.description:
Kamal Sharma

Security leader Kamal Sharma shares what a day in the life of a modern security leader looks like - from managing teams and priorities to navigating boardroom conversations and incidents. A conversation about leadership, craft, and the realities of running security at scale.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1TzM_2dUDcE

item.title: SERIES 2 - Leadership Conversation with Divakar Prayaga
item.description:
Divakar Prayaga

A leadership conversation with Divakar Prayaga, exploring his experience across security leadership and entrepreneurship. Divakar talks about his career path, his approach to leading teams, and what it takes to lead effectively in the cyber space.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=L57kpbiIftI

item.title: SERIES 2 - Episode 06 - Vivek Ramachandran
item.description:
Vivek Ramachandran - SquareX

Founder of SquareX and long-time security researcher, author, and entrepreneur. Vivek shares his journey from wireless security research to founding multiple security businesses, and what running a security startup looks like today.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eIcImWol1KY

item.title: SERIES 2 - Episode 05 - Sumit Siddharth
item.description:
Sumit Siddharth

https://www.linkedin.com/in/sumsid/

Bio:-

Cyber Entrepreneur| CEO -The SecOps Group| Angel investor, advisor, mentor to Startups.

Learning for Mentees:-

item.title: SERIES 2 - Episode 04 - Ankita Gupta
item.description:
Ankita Gupta - Akto

Co-founder of Akto. Ankita shares her journey into founding an API security company, the lessons she has learned about building and scaling a product, and what drives her as an entrepreneur in the security space.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rIZOAWGtsbs

item.title: SERIES 2 - Episode 03 - Ankita Dhakar
item.description:
Ankita Dhakar - Security Lit

Founder of Security Lit. Ankita talks about building a cybersecurity company from the ground up, her path into entrepreneurship, and what a day looks like as a founder navigating the security startup space.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nyGe0TO90Ks

item.title: SERIES 2 - Episode 02 - Travis McPeak
item.description:
Travis McPeak

Cloud security leader, founder, and long-time AppSec practitioner. Travis shares his journey from enterprise security roles into founding a cloud security company, and his perspective on building products developers actually want to use.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VQgjogpVpFM

item.title: SERIES 2 - Episode 01 - Lakshmi Das
item.description:
Lakshmi Das

Opening episode of Series 2 of “A Day in the Life of an Entrepreneur”. Lakshmi shares her journey into security entrepreneurship, the challenges she has navigated, and the lessons she has learned while building in the cyber space.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EBAumGwJTWQ

item.title: Episode 26 - Marina Segal
item.description:
Marina Segal

Cybersecurity founder and product leader. Marina talks about building a cyber security company, the challenges of fundraising and scaling, and what she has learned about leadership and resilience as an entrepreneur.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XIvMVJbB6pY

item.title: Episode 25 - Vikram Mehta
item.description:
Vikram Mehta

Cybersecurity leader and entrepreneur. Vikram shares his experience of building and scaling security work in modern enterprises, and the mindset shifts required to step from a corporate security role into entrepreneurship.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lgJlb2aEZNU

item.title: Episode 24 - Rahul Tyagi
item.description:
Rahul Tyagi

Cybersecurity entrepreneur, author, and educator. Rahul shares his journey into security training and entrepreneurship, how he thinks about building a community-first business, and what daily life looks like as a founder in cyber.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sSOam04N9w4

item.title: Episode 23 - Sandeep Kamble
item.description:
Sandeep Kamble

Founder and cybersecurity entrepreneur. Sandeep talks about the origin story of his company, the realities of running a security startup, and the lessons he has picked up about leadership and resilience as an entrepreneur.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Jw2OarwrAzk

item.title: Episode 22 - Muslim Koser
item.description:
Muslim Koser

Cybersecurity leader and entrepreneur. Muslim shares his path into security leadership and entrepreneurship, what a typical day looks like running security programmes and initiatives, and how he approaches building teams and products.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sqEUIrLZ0jU

item.title: Episode 21 - Akshat Jain
item.description:
Akshat Jain

Cybersecurity entrepreneur. Akshat talks about his journey into founding a security business, the challenges of getting a startup off the ground, and the lessons he has learned along the way.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lIxvNIUfk_Q

item.title: Episode 20 - Kunal Bajaj and Sachin Kumar
item.description:
Kunal Bajaj and Sachin Kumar

Co-founders in the cybersecurity space. In this conversation, Kunal and Sachin share their paths into entrepreneurship, how they built their company together, and what a typical day looks like running a security business as co-founders.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xNW8bZmQwho

item.title: Episode 19 - Rahul Sasi
item.description:
Rahul Sasi

Cybersecurity researcher and founder. Rahul shares his journey from vulnerability research to founding a security product company, the highs and lows of building a security startup, and what drives him as an entrepreneur in the cyber space.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wALCZs7xUEY

item.title: Episode 18 - Ed Adams
item.description:
Ed Adams

CEO and software security entrepreneur. Ed shares his long career in application security, what it takes to build and run a security training and services company, and how he thinks about leadership, culture, and the human side of software security.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bh2HVp7yYCI

item.title: Episode 17 - Arun Mane
item.description:
Arun Mane

IoT and hardware security researcher and entrepreneur. Arun talks about his journey into hardware security, building a company focused on IoT and automotive security, and what a typical day looks like running a specialised security firm.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eXIwKq1tifk

item.title: Episode 16 - Tanya Janca
item.description:
Tanya Janca (SheHacksPurple)

Application security expert, author, educator, and founder. Tanya shares her journey from developer to AppSec leader to running her own security education business, and the day-to-day realities of being an entrepreneur in the DevSecOps and AppSec space.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QXpyKM0FJ8M

item.title: Episode 15 - Ken Underhill
item.description:
Ken Underhill

Cybersecurity educator, entrepreneur, and media creator. Ken shares his story of building security education at scale, what a day looks like running a cyber training and content business, and how he balances teaching, entrepreneurship, and community.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BTBaEOCfzb8

item.title: Episode 14 - Kumar Ritesh
item.description:
Kumar Ritesh

Cybersecurity entrepreneur and founder leading threat intelligence innovation. Kumar talks about his path from intelligence roles into building a cyber threat intelligence company, and the lessons he has learned while scaling a security-first business.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IBiSMndi3Uo

item.title: Episode 13 - Grant Ongers
item.description:
Grant Ongers

Entrepreneur, security leader, and long-time OWASP community contributor. In this conversation, Grant shares his journey into security entrepreneurship, the hurdles along the way, and what a typical day looks like leading security work across organisations and communities.

Reference:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AChM3Ocj00w

item.title: Episode 12 - Pawan Desai
item.description:
Pawan Desai

https://www.linkedin.com/in/pawan-desai-5455562/

https://twitter.com/pawandesai

Bio:-

Co-founder & CEO - MitKat Advisory Services, Cyber Security, IVLP fellow, Submariner, Advisor AllMiles, Visiting faculty at NMIMS MitKat Risk Intelligence and Monitoring Engine, Certified Corporate Director

Learning for Mentees:

  1. Do what you love

  2. Mediocrity will kill the culture, Don’t accept Mediocrity, Work with people who want to work for a cause

  3. Your work doesn’t define you. To be known for is who and what you are.

item.title: Episode 11 - Aseem Jakhar
item.description:
Aseem Jakhar

https://www.linkedin.com/in/aseemjakhar/

https://twitter.com/aseemjakhar

Bio:-

Entrepreneur | IoT security | Co-Founder - Payatu, hardwear.io, nullcon.net, null.co.in | Author - EXPLIoT framework

Learning for Mentees:

Try, Fail, Try Again - You will win

item.title: Episode 10 - Shubham Mittal
item.description:
Shubham Mittal

https://www.linkedin.com/in/upgoingstar/ https://twitter.com/upgoingstar

Bio:-

Co-Founder at RedHunt Labs | BlackHat Asia Review Board Member

Learning for Mentees:-

item.title: Episode 9 - Aaron Rinehart
item.description:
Aaron Rinehart

https://twitter.com/aaronrinehart

https://www.linkedin.com/in/aaronsrinehart/

Bio:-

Aaron has been expanding the possibilities of Chaos Engineering in its application to other safety- critical portions of the IT domain notably cybersecurity. He began pioneering the application of Security in Chaos Engineering during his tenure as the Chief Security Architect at the largest private healthcare company in the world, UnitedHealth Group (UHG). While at UHG Aaron released ChaoSlingr, one of the first open source software releases focused on using Chaos Engineering in cybersecurity to build more resilient systems. Aaron recently founded the first Chaos Engineering Meetup in Washington DC and is a frequent author, consultant and speaker in the space.

item.title: Episode 7 - Dhillon Kannabhiran
item.description:
Dhillon Kannabhiran

https://www.linkedin.com/in/l33tdawg/

Bio:-

Dhillon Andrew Kannabhiran (@l33tdawg on Twitter) is the Founder and Chief Executive Officer of Hack in The Box (http://www.hitb.org), organiser of the HITBSecConf series of network security conferences which has been held annually for over a decade in various countries including Malaysia, The Netherlands and the UAE.

Prior to quitting his day job to lead the HITB team on crazy adventures around the world, Dhillon started off at the height of the dotcom craze as a technology journalist with PC World, ZDnet, MIS Asia and CNet. When the bubble burst, he moved on to a Malaysian telco as Chief IT Officer to spend his days in the world of Cisco AS5300s, in a land of packet switched networks at a time when Asterisk did not just mean.

item.title: Episode 8 - Amar Singh
item.description:
Amar Singh

https://www.linkedin.com/in/chiefinfosec/

Bio:-

Amar Singh is a CEO, CISO, Cyber Crisis & Incident Response Practitioner, Speaker & Co-Founder Cyber Management Alliance.

Amar is an industry acknowledged expert and public speaker and is regularly invited to speak and share his insights by some of the largest and most respected organisations in the world including The BBC, The Economist’s Intelligence Unit, The Financial Times, SC Magazine, InfoSec Magazine, Computer Weekly, The Register and the Al-Jazeera English Channel.

item.title: Episode 6 - Keenan Skelly
item.description:
Keenan Skelly

https://www.linkedin.com/in/keenan-skelly/

Bio:-

• Co-Founder & CEO - shyftED, Inc.

• President and CEO - Spark Security Solutions, Inc

• Strategic Advisor to CEO - Circadence Corp.

• Director, Board of Directors – Night Lion Security

• Advisor, Women in Cybersecurity Advisory Council – SC Media

• Games Master - Women’s Society of Cyberjutsu

Ms. Skelly has twenty years’ experience in providing security and management solutions across a wide array of platforms to include personnel, physical, and cyber security. She brings more than ten years in government service with a focus on National and Homeland Security. Ms. Skelly served in the US Army as an Explosive Ordnance Disposal Technician and went on to work for the Department of Homeland Security where she served as Chief for Comprehensive Reviews in the Office for Infrastructure Protection. In this capacity she ran vulnerability assessments and exercises on Critical Infrastructure assets throughout the Nation, and helped to develop the first systems assessment approach for Critical Infrastructure and Key Resources.

item.title: Episode 5 - Mari Galloway
item.description:
Mari Galloway

https://www.linkedin.com/in/themarigalloway/

https://twitter.com/marigalloway

Bio:-

Mari is the CEO and a founding board member for the Women’s Society of Cyberjutsu (WSC), one of the fastest growing 501c3 non-profit cybersecurity communities dedicated to bringing more women and girls to cyber. WSC provides its members with the resources and support required to enter and advance as a cybersecurity professional. Mari began her cyber career with Accenture where she excelled as a Network Engineer. Mari is also the inaugural ISC2 Diversity Award winner for 2019. With over 11 years of Information Technology, 8 of which are in cybersecurity, her experience spans network design and security architecture, risk assessments, vulnerability management, incident response and policy development across government and commercial industries. She holds a variety of technical and management certifications (CISSP, GIAC, CCNA, etc) as well as a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Information Systems from Columbus State University and a Master of Science in Information Systems from Strayer University.

item.title: Episode 4 - Takaharu Ogasa
item.description:
Takaharu Ogasa https://twitter.com/TakaharuOgasa https://www.linkedin.com/in/takaharu-ogasa-a245b7b5/

Bio: Takaharu is the Founder of Security Initiative, a start-up and a Cyber Security consultancy. He is also the chapter leader for OWASP Sandai Chapter.

Learning for the Mentees:-

Link to the Previous Episodes: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R75Oye_LIaI&list=PLUKo5k_oSrfMzA0_VbOJjKh1AViSYcNrr

item.title: Episode 3 - Bryson Bort
item.description:
Bio:

Bryson is the Founder of SCYTHE, a start-up building a next generation attack emulation platform, and GRIMM, a cybersecurity consultancy, and Co-Founder of the ICS Village, a non-profit advancing awareness of industrial control system security. He is a Senior Fellow for Cybersecurity and National Security at R Street and the National Security Institute and an Advisor to the Army Cyber Institute. As a U.S. Army Officer, he served as a Battle Captain and Brigade Engineering Officer in support of Operation Iraqi Freedom before leaving the Army as a Captain. He was recognized as one of the Top 50 in Cyber in 2020 by Business Insider.

item.title: Episode 2 - Shira Shamban
item.description:
Shira Shamban

https://twitter.com/ShambanIT​

Bio:

Shira Shamban is a security researcher and technical expert with a focus on cloud security. Currently, she is the co-founder and CEO of Solvo. Shira started her professional career in #cybersecurity​ as a military officer in the elite intelligence unit 8200 of the Israel Defense Force. During her 13-year service in the unit, Shira acquired hands-on experience in cybersecurity and intelligence operations while earning an engineering degree from Tel-Aviv University.

item.title: Episode 1 - Shivangi Nadkarni
item.description:
Shivani Nadkarni (Co-founder & CEO - Arrka)

https://twitter.com/shivanginadkarn​

Bio: Shivangi has over 24 years of experience in the domains of Information Security & Risk Management, E-Commerce & Networking across multiple geographies. She has earlier worked with organizations like Wipro & Sify handling a variety of roles and heading different Lines of Business. She was instrumental in setting up the first licensed Certifying Authority in India – in association with VeriSign. She has authored the Privacy Book of Knowledge (PBoK) for DSCI for their DCPP certification program. Shivangi is a regular speaker, writer and faculty at various forums and programs. Shivangi holds a Bachelor’s degree in Electrical & Electronics Engineering from BITS, Pilani and Master’s degree in Management from the Indian Institute of Management, Calcutta. She is also a CISA, CIPT and DCPP.
